calendar.matters:
FUTURE ACCOUNTING
TENTATIVE RULING: Hearing. Appearance required. Contrary to Counsel's assertion, the petition was not filed "by Friday" after the hearing or even as of 6/3/26.
Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk.
Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME.
Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall.
Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances.
For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879.
For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
EXAMINER: HELEN TROWBRIDGE

FUTURE ACCOUNTING

Counsel stated that he could have the filing in by Friday. The matter is continued to June 23, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Bloom. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar May-14-2024 Continued To Jun-18-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jay Jambeck appeared by zoom for petitioner.

Mr. Jambeck stated that the reason for the non-appearance had to do with a calendaring issue. Santions in the amount of $250.00 are ordered against Mr. Jambeck. The court noted that it still does not have the final inventory and appraisal showing the assets as of February 6, 2023. Mr. Jambeck confirmed that the amount in the account is $72,500.00, stated that nothing is going out to the beneficiary as of this accounting, and that he understands that the conservator is moving this account to an interest-bearing account. The First Account and Report of Trustee is continued to June 18, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the final inventory and appraisal is also set for June 18, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Jambeck is to file the inventory and appraisal by June 4, 2024.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Jambeck (attorney) to appear on May 14, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear. Status first account and report of trustee is also continued to that date.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Oct-25-2023 Continued To Dec-06-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Jay Jambeck, Esq. for Juan Ramos and Mandy Leigh, Trustees/Petitioners;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

The Court stated that an accounting was recently filed and inquired about status on the inventory and appraisal. Mr. Jambeck stated that they just deposited the funds and that they can get the inventory and appraisal filed in a couple of days. The Court stated that it understands that it took time for the minor's comp, that it would have happily continued the status dates if counsel had asked, and that it can be frustrating for staff and the Court to prepare for a hearing and then no one appear. Mr. Jambeck stated that it was purely there issue. The Court ordered Mr. Jambeck to pay sanctions in the amount of $500.00 within 10 days. The Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause are off calendar.

Today's status on filing the inventory and appraisal is continued to December 6, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: R. Cabrera. Matter not reported.

The Court issues sanctions in the amount of $250.00 against Jay T. Jambeck Pursuant to CCP 177.5 payable within 10 days of the order.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Jay T. Jambeck (attorney) to appear on October 25, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $500.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear. Status hearing on filing I&A is also continued to that date.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Maloney: Not reported.

Petition For An Order 1. Establishing A Special Needs Trust; 2. For Authority To Invest In Mutual Funds And Government Bonds With Maturity Dates Later Than Five Years
Off Calendar Feb-06-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jay Jambeck, Esq., via Zoom, for and with Clarissa Ignacio and Juan Ramos (Petitioners/Parents/Guardians ad Litem), both appearing via Zoom.

Mr. Jambeck stated that they did amend, that the filing was rejected because there was no "form" notice, that he needs to renotice and refile, and that the filing is ready to go. The Court inquired about the nature of the amendment. Mr. Jambeck stated that the filing simply acknowledges that the petitioners are the minor's parents and the guardians ad litem. The Court stated that it had looked at the file, that bond would be fixed at $79,750.00 which includes a 10% recovery, that the petitioners' standing as the guardians ad litem is confirmed, and that it is prepared to proceed. The Court inquired if anyone was appearing to object. There being no objections, the Petition for Order Establishing SNT and Authority to Invest in Mutual Funds is taken under submission with the intent to grant. The Court noted that the order will be revised.

A signed copy of the trust order and the bond are to be filed on or before February 15, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
Status on the filing of the Inventory and Appraisal is set for May 10, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
Status on the filing of the First Account and Status Report is set for February 14, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

**After the hearing, the Court vacated the status dates given in open court and set the following future dates:
Status on the filing of a signed copy of the trust instrument with a signed copy of the trust order and status on the filing of the trustee's bond are set for March 1, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
Status on the filing of the Inventory and Appraisal is set for July 5, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
Status on the filing of the First Account and Status Report is set for April 20, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. (Please note that date of April 20, 2024, is a Saturday. Status on the filing of the First Account is reset to April 24, 2024.) **

Petition For An Order 1. Establishing A Special Needs Trust; 2. For Authority To Invest In Mutual Funds And Government Bonds With Maturity Dates Later Than Five Years
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Continued To Dec-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Jay Jambeck, Esq. for Juan Ramos and Clarissa Ignacio (Petitioners). Clarissa Ignacio appeared via Zoom.

The Court stated that the amount of the Special Needs Trust is below the minimum, that the Guardian ad Litem (GAL) needs to file the petition, and that both parents have not been appointed. Mr. Jambeck stated that their minor's comp petition pending in Dept. 514 was continued to January 24th, that he filed an ex parte petition for appointment of a GAL in October, and that that they are still waiting on a ruling on the ex parte petition. Brief discussions regarding the ex parte petition are held. Mr. Jambeck was advised to submit a hard copy of the ex parte petition for appointment of a GAL which was filed in October to the Probate Director. Discussions conclude.

The Petition for Order Establishing a Special Needs Trust and for Authority to Invest in Mutual Funds and Government Bonds is continued to December 28, 2022, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Jambeck is to give notice of the December 28th hearing to the health care agencies.
Brief discussions regarding Mr. Jambeck's proof of service are held. Mr. Jambeck is allowed to use the same form for his proof of service.

Judge: Cecelia Castellanos. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Lozado. Matter not reported.

Petition For An Order 1. Establishing A Special Needs Trust; 2. For Authority To Invest In Mutual Funds And Government Bonds With Maturity Dates Later Than Five Years
Off Calendar Oct-18-2022 Continued To Nov-29-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jay Jambeck, via Zoom, for and with Juan Ramos and Clarissa Ignacio, Petitioners.

The court confirms that counsel plans to move forward with the Special Needs Trust (SNT) and of a pending petition for Minors Compromise. Counsel advises of having e-filed notice to the three (3) government agencies as discussed at the last calling of this matter. Counsel is advised that his notice has been rejected by the clerk's office. The court advises counsel regarding not delivering a courtesy copy of the petition and proposed order to the court and is advised to deliver the documents by the next calling of this matter or the court may consider taking the matter off calendar.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Nov-29-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is reminded to deliver courtesy copies of the petition, a proposed order thereon, and a proof of service to the court by the next hearing date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Petition For An Order 1. Establishing A Special Needs Trust; 2. For Authority To Invest In Mutual Funds And Government Bonds With Maturity Dates Later Than Five Years
Off Calendar Sep-27-2022 Continued To Oct-18-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jay Jambeck, via Zoom, for Juan Ramos and Clarissa Ignacio, Petitioners.

The court and counsel discuss the petition and whether counsel wishes to procced in light of the current asset limit to establish a Special Needs Trust (SNT) and advises counsel that if he does wish to proceed in establishing the SNT he will be required to provide service to the three (3) government agencies under Probate Code, submit a copy of the petition, and a proposed order to the court by the next calling of this matter.

The matter is continued to Oct-18-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Cecilia Castellanos. Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.
